title: _index.md structural contract
description: Machine-readable contract for what every _index.md must contain. Consumed by the post-write hook (Tier 1 live-sync + auto-bootstrap), the librarian index-maintain capability (Tier 2 audit sweep + Tier 3 --deep semantic validation), and any future tooling that needs to validate _index.md compliance. PLACEHOLDER — fields are specified at contract level; runtime details (exact sentinel marker syntax, exact enum lookup mechanism, exact path-parsing logic) bind at implementation time in SP05.

parent_folder.rationale: parent_folder is the navigation-side lineage pointer; omitted at depth 1 because vault-root-direct folders have no folder-parent in the meaningful sense
parent_folder.depth_derivation: PLACEHOLDER — derived from the file's path relative to vault root; depth 1 = `<cluster>/_index.md`; depth ≥ 2 = `<cluster>/<instance>/_index.md` or deeper

type.rationale: Hooks branch on type; type:index activates _index.md validation logic

parent_folder.rationale: Relative path from vault root naming the parent folder of this _index.md. Auto-populated by the post-write hook at bootstrap (Tier 1 step 2); librarian index-maintain audits for path-vs-frontmatter drift at Tier 2/3.

tags.rationale: Folder-mirrors-tag invariant. Tag matches the folder's structural-dimension lineage.

updated.rationale: ISO-8601 date; bumped by every maintenance pass on the file

description.rationale: Optional one-line scope description; useful for downstream librarian summaries

provides.rationale: Optional cross-folder grep handle (R-39). NOT surfaced in the contents-enum table; lives in frontmatter only as a queryable handle for cross-folder discovery.

h1.match_rule: PLACEHOLDER — exact match or close variant of the folder name
h1.rationale: Matches the folder name or close variant. Reinforces folder-mirrors-tag invariant — the heading reads like a tag value.

folder_context_paragraph.rationale: Load-bearing pedagogy content. Without it, the index is regeneratable boilerplate; humans skip it because `ls` produces the same information; LLMs gain nothing from reading it. NOT auto-generated — adopter or operator authors the paragraph; bootstrap hook emits a placeholder for fill-in.

sentinel_markers.rationale: Automated maintenance (Tier 1 hook + Tier 2 librarian) touches only content between markers; preserves hand-authored prose outside (folder-context paragraph, cross-references).

columns.name: File
columns.rationale: Folder navigation; the unambiguous identifier; clickable in any Obsidian reader

columns.name: Lines
columns.derivation: wc -l on the referenced file at maintenance time
columns.rationale: Cost-to-load signal — token-budget approximation before Claude decides to read it

columns.name: Type
columns.derivation: extracted from the referenced file's frontmatter `type:` field
columns.rationale: Structural role identifier — distinguishes the engagement's overview from reference from people without needing separate sectioning; R-32-enforced via vault-schema.json

columns.name: Description
columns.derivation: extracted from the referenced file's H1, `description:` frontmatter field, or first body paragraph (in that fallback order)
columns.rationale: What reading this file gives you in plain language

ordering_convention.rationale: Key-role types listed before supporting types makes the load-priority signal explicit; convention not enforced as DENY — surfaces in librarian Tier 3 --deep audit so adopters can hand-tune ordering when convention doesn't fit.

light_content_fallback.condition: folder carries fewer than PLACEHOLDER (target ~3) child files of distinct types
light_content_fallback.alternative: prose 'Current Contents' section replaces the table
light_content_fallback.rationale: Table enumeration is premature when the folder has very few children; prose is more readable

exempt_path_categories: scraper aggregation surfaces (Inbox/ — uses inbox-index-refresh capability, not index-maintain)
non-vault infrastructure (Tags/ make-md artifacts; _orchestrator/ plan-tree state; tests/fixtures/)
exemption_list_pointer.rationale: Single source of truth for which folders are exempt from _index.md mandate. The post-write hook + librarian index-maintain consume from there at runtime — when a write hits an exempt folder, the hook does not auto-create _index.md and the librarian does not flag missing-index as drift.

consumers.name: post-write-verify.sh
consumers.role: Tier 1 live-sync + auto-bootstrap on Edit/Write tool calls
consumers.loop_guard: if written file path matches *_index.md → exit 0 before any work

consumers.name: index-maintain.sh
consumers.role: Tier 2 audit sweep (auto-fix missing/orphan/drift); Tier 3 --deep semantic validation (description-vs-H1, ordering coherence)
consumers.implementation_target: new file at skills/librarian/capabilities/index-maintain.sh; SP05 implements per the SP03-authors-contract / SP05-implements pattern used for governance-parity-audit

tier_1_live_sync.trigger: PostToolUse hook fires on Edit/Write tool calls
tier_1_live_sync.scope: single folder of the written file
tier_1_live_sync.writes: auto-bootstrap (if missing + non-exempt) + reconcile entry for written file + bump updated:

tier_2_audit_sweep.scope: every non-exempt folder in the vault
tier_2_audit_sweep.writes: auto-fix missing entries + remove orphan rows + correct drifted line counts and types + bump updated:
tier_2_audit_sweep.blast_radius: every _index.md in the vault that has drift

tier_3_deep_audit.writes: auto-corrects mechanical drift (line counts, type values); FLAGS semantic drift (description-vs-content mismatch, ordering incoherence) for operator review — does NOT auto-overwrite hand-tuned content
tier_3_deep_audit.blast_radius: same as Tier 2 + advisory findings emitted via librarian audit log
